LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GARY CATRON'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES. A R G U E D. THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART. MR. CATRON IS AWARDED ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $11,400 AGAINST PLAINTIFF KORAY ERGUR. WHILE MR. CATRON IS ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF FEES, HIS REQUEST FOR MORE THAN $34,000 IN FEES IS EXCESSIVE IN LIGHT OF THE ISSUES INVOLVED. WHILE MR. CATRON WAS FREE TO HAVE HIS ATTORNEYS WORK AS MANY HOURS AS HE DESIRED ON HIS ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AND THIS MOTION, THE DETERMINATION OF THE AMOUNT OF FEES IS BASED ON THE NUMBER OF HOURS THAT WERE REASONABLY NECESSARY FOR THE TWO MOTIONS. IN ADDITION, MR. CATRON SEEKS FEES FOR WORK THAT WAS NOT DIRECTLY RELATED TO EITHER MOTION. (SEE, E.G., 4/6, 4/11 AND 4/12 ENTRIES IN EXHIBIT A TO MR. HOROWITT'S DECLARATION). MR. CATRON SEEKS FEES BASED ON THE WORK OF SEVERAL TIMEKEEPERS INCLUDING A PARALEGAL. THE AVERAGE HOURLY RATE SOUGHT IS $380, WHICH IS REASONABLE FOR THE WORK DONE BY COUNSEL FOR MR. CATRON. BASED ON THE COURT'S OWN KNOWLEDGE OF THIS CASE AND OF THE TIME REQUIRED FOR MR. CATRON'S MOTIONS, THE COURT DETERMINES THAT NO MORE THAN 30 HOURS OF WORK FOR EXPERIENCED COUNSEL AT THE BLENDED RATE OF $380 PER HOUR WAS REASONABLY NECESSARY FOR MR. CATRON'S ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AND THIS MOTION, PARTICULARLY SINCE MR. CATRON HAD THE BENEFIT OF AN ORDER GRANTING ANTI-SLAPP MOTION FILED BY OTHER DEFENDANTS. 30 X $380 IS $11,400. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189

Defendant Gary Catrons Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Mar-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Koray Ergur, in pro per, Tel. 415.938.6813, for plaintiffs;
Darryl J. Horowitt, of Coleman & Horowitt, LLP, Tel. 559.248.4820, for defendants Gary Catron, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT GARY CATRON's Motion For Attorneys Fees is granted in part. Mr. Catron is awarded attorney's fees in the amount of $11,400 against plaintiff Koray Ergur. While Mr. Catron is entitled to an award of fees, his request for more than $34,000 in fees is excessive in light of the issues involved. While Mr. Catron was free to have his attorneys work as many hours as he desired on his anti-SLAPP motion and this motion, the determination of the amount of fees is based on the number of hours that were reasonably necessary for the two motions. In addition, Mr. Catron seeks fees for work that was not directly related to either motion. (See, e.g., 4/6, 4/11 and 4/12 entries in exhibit A to Mr. Horowitt's declaration). Mr. Catron seeks fees based on the work of several timekeepers including a paralegal. The average hourly rate sought is $380, which is reasonable for the work done by counsel for Mr. Catron. Based on the court's own knowledge of this case and of the time required for Mr. Catron's motions, the court determines that no more than 30 hours of work for experienced counsel at the blended rate of $380 per hour was reasonably necessary for Mr. Catron's anti-SLAPP motion and this motion, particularly since Mr. Catron had the benefit of an order granting anti-SLAPP motion filed by other defendants. 30 x $380 is $11,400. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Defendants California Mortgage & Realty, Inc., David Choo, Henry Park, James Gala And Myxolidian, Llc'S Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 Of Defendants, California Mortgage And Realty, Inc., David Choo, Henry Park, James Gala, And Myxolidian, Llc.
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Koray Ergur (415.938.6813) appears as plaintiff/opposing party, In Propria Persona

Attorney, Jeffery D. Trowbridge of Towbridge Law Office (510.893.5300) appears as counsel dor defendants/moving parties

Attorney, Douglas C. Straus of Archer Norris Law Firm (925.930.6600) appears as counsel for defendants, TMG Partners and Michael Covarrubias

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants California Mortgage & Realty, Inc., David Choo, Henry Park, James Gala And Myxolidian, Llc'S Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 is GRANTED and the moving parties are awarded $17,250 in attorney's fees and costs. The moving defendants satisfied their burden of showing that all of the claims alleged against them by plaintiff Koray Ergur arises in substantial part from protected activity and Mr. Ergur has not shown the required minimal merit of any of his claims. All of Mr. Ergur's claims against the moving defendants concern real property and sale, loan and foreclosure transactions that were involved in bankruptcy proceedings and resulted in orders issued by the bankruptcy court. Moving defendants directly or indirectly participated in those proceedings. In his papers in opposition to this motion, Mr. Ergur acknowledges this when he states in paragraph 25 of his declaration that "Although the Bankruptcy Court approved a compromise that significantly rewarded ' David Choo with millions of dollars, I was unaware of their scam, left penniless and without any of the properties. Even the Bankruptcy Court was misled by a sophisticated and highly skilled group of men [apparently referring to Mr. Choo and the other individual moving defendants among others] who successfully acquired the subject property through a complex scam'" Per the second prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis, Mr. Ergur has the burden of showing minimal merit of his claims. He has not done so. All the evidence submitted by the parties shows that all of Mr. Ergur's claims against all of the moving parties accrued no later than 2009, more than four years before this lawsuit was filed and thus all of those claims are time-barred. Moreover, the evidence submitted by the parties shows that all of the claims alleged by Mr. Ergur against the moving parties were within the exclusive jurisdiction of the bankruptcy court and thus Mr. Ergur may not prosecute those claims in this court. As the prevailing parties on this motion, the moving parties are entitled to an award of reasonable attorney's fees and costs. The court further finds that a reasonable amount of attorney time for this motion is 40 hours. At a reasonable hourly blended rate of $375 per hour, the court awards attorney fees of $15,000. In addition, the moving parties are entitled to costs of $2,250. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Koray Ergur (415.938.6813) appears as plaintiff/opposing party, In Propria Persona

Attorney, Jeffery D. Trowbridge of Towbridge Law Office (510.893.5300) appears as counsel dor defendants, California Mortgage & Realty, David Chu, ET AL.

Attorney, Douglas C. Straus of Archer Norris Law Firm (925.930.6600) appears as counsel for defendants/Moving Parties, TMG Partners and Michael Covarrubias

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants Michael Covarrubias And Tmg Partners' Motion For Attorney Fees And Expenses

Defendants Michael Covarrubias and TMG Partners' motion for attorney's fees is GRANTED in part. Mr. Covarrubias and TMG partners are awarded attorney's fees and cost in the amount of $25,670. As the prevailing parties on their anti-SLAPP motion, Mr. Covarrubias and TMG Partners are entitled to an award of attorney's fees and costs. While the court does not doubt that counsel for Mr. Covarrubias and TMG Partners expended the time they say they did on the anti-SLAPP motion and this motion, given the nature of the issues on these two motions and the fact that plaintiff is representing himself, the court finds that the number of attorney hours for which the moving defendants seek recovery is excessive and not reasonable. Moreover, the court has not been provided with an explanation why both the Archer Norris firm and Mr. Verges worked as attorneys on the anti-SLAPP motion and thus finds that the fees charged by Mr. Verges for his work cannot be recovered per the anti-SLAPP statutes. While the court appreciates that Mr. Verges was the client contact and provided information to the Archer Norris firm for the anti-SLAPP motion, serving in that role is distinct from serving as an attorney whose time is compensable under the anti-SLAPP statute The court further finds that a reasonable amount of attorney time for the anti-SLAPP motion and this motion is 60 hours. At a reasonable hourly blended rate of $425 per hour, the court awards attorney fees of $25,500. In addition, the moving parties are entitled to costs of $170. No explanation was provided why paralegal time was expended on either motion and thus no fees are awarded for paralegal work. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
